original_hypothesis.title: RollupCore.deleteStaker swap-and-pop can corrupt staker index mapping if duplicated address appears in _stakerList
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: deleteStaker assumes `_stakerList` contains unique staker addresses and that each `_stakerMap[addr].index` matches its position. If, due to any upstream bug (e.g., multiple createNewStake for same address via reentrancy or initialization corruption), `_stakerList` contains duplicates, swap-and-pop can update the wrong mapping entry and corrupt indices, potentially breaking future operations that rely on staker indices.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Uniqueness is enforced by `require(!isStaked(msg.sender))` in _newStake, but a deeper audit should confirm no alternative path can insert duplicates. Index corruption is a high-leverage state bug for staking systems.
deployment_assumptions: A separate bug allows duplicate insertion into _stakerList or inconsistent _stakerMap indices (e.g., via unexpected reentrancy or admin logic not shown)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Admin logic and full proxy wiring not included; cannot fully prove uniqueness enforcement across all upgrade/admin entry points.

proof_of_signal.pattern: swap-and-pop relies on uniqueness/integrity of index mapping

preconditions: _stakerList contains a duplicate or _stakerMap index mismatch exists

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: array+mapping index coupling without invariant assertions
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: state corruption leading to inability to manage stakes correctly
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no assert(_stakerList[stakerIndex] == stakerAddress) precondition checks; no uniqueness invariant enforcement on list

analysis_questions: Is there any path besides createNewStake that can mutate _stakerList/_stakerMap?
Can reentrancy during token transfers cause createNewStake to be called twice before isStaked flips?
Do admin logic contracts (not provided) have maintenance functions that edit stakers?
validation_requirements: Search codebase for any writes to _stakerList/_stakerMap besides createNewStake/deleteStaker
Add invariants: _stakerList[_stakerMap[a].index] == a for all stakers
Reentrancy tests around newStake/addToDeposit token transfer hooks

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If a duplicate staker entry is introduced, attacker triggers returnOldDeposit to remove one entry, causing index corruption and potentially locking other stakers or misrouting withdrawals.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potential loss or lock of staking funds due to corrupted bookkeeping
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All stakers if global list corruption occurs

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No invariant-based tests for staker list/index consistency

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: on-chain invariant checker can detect index mismatches

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
